claims.claim: Illmari Vaino is the founder of Iconoclasm.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Gin Soaked Rag is associated with the Sin and Tonic.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Dura Oil Drinker is a mechanic associated with Iconoclasm.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Elias Ramsey serves as the quartermaster of Iconoclasm.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: The Roundhat Gang is run by Tywelwyn Leatherhide.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: The faction known as Pyrelight is associated with a front company called Embercall Industries.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Tywelwyn Leatherhide ordered materials from Embercall Industries, the front company of the Pyrelight faction.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The local law enforcement in the area is called the Threshold Authority, also known as the TA.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Augers are creatures created through a divination ritual and can be fed to enhance divination.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: The Zorbon is a subject of conspiratorial belief, with claims that it is connected to mushrooms and radio signals, and that tinfoil hats provide protection from it.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: A Fane is a structure whose existence and size are sustained by a magical object called a heart.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The size of a Fane is directly related to the amount of magical energy available to the heart sustaining it.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Multiple hearts can jointly maintain a single Fane, a practice used by large organizational headquarters to sustain large footprints.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Belvedere has integrated hearts as an additional tier within its social or civic hierarchy, and Fanes are more common there than in most other places.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Concordia is a major corporation in this setting, functioning as the equivalent of real-world consumer-electronics and appliance manufacturers such as LG, GE, or Samsung.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Concordia works to ensure that all of its consumer-facing hearts share consistent branding and a uniform appearance.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The RunGunners are a particularly famous group of mercenaries and guns-for-hire.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The RunGunners maintain a city in the middle of a desert that is sustained almost entirely by one very large Fane, because no other means of supporting a city in that desert exists.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The RunGunners' desert city is sustained by many hearts of completely different personalities, resulting in a constantly clashing, mishmash environment.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: When hearts of differing origins are combined in a single Fane, the areas where they meet appear strange and patchwork rather than seamless.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Ginny's appearance consists of a suit vest and dress pants, but instead of a head, Ginny has a twisted-up dish rag.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Illmari is normally the cook at the establishment where Ginny works the bar.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Ginny works the bar and is capable of cooking, though he has not mastered cooking human food as well as Illmari has.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The twins are not running a magic item shop, but are two people who are particularly good at crafting and can make healing potions if provided with the necessary components.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The NPC craftsman's workshop is where he makes rocket hammers, and he considers it a particularly dangerous place to activate the radar dish.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The radar dish is believed to be a device for receiving a signal.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Anouk keeps a Rolodex-like collection of index cards containing contacts and job leads, which she uses to refer people to work opportunities.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: There is a scrapyard located one district over from Anouk's location, in the district of Sableclutch.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Anouk has an old friend who runs a scrapyard in Sableclutch and regularly hires couriers and other workers, preferring to engage individuals directly rather than through a courier service.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Arbiters are empowered by the The Scale to conduct bounty hunting, and the The Scale does not extend this authority to unaffiliated individuals.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Three police organizations operate in the city of Hallia, and each typically offers bounties for outstanding warrants.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: In Hallia, particularly strong magic set up by the host prevents people from teleporting or sliding between city districts freely; residents must use public transit to travel between neighborhoods.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Hallia has a trolley or tram system that travels along a route called 'the Horizon', connecting different districts including Star 12.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The district of Star 12 is located in the starward direction along the Horizon tram line in Hallia.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The neighborhood where the Sin and Tonic is located is one of the poorer parts of Hallia, characterized as a commercialized area where people earn modest incomes.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The starward districts of Hallia feature older, poorly maintained buildings that were constructed when the tram line was first built, in anticipation of a boom that never materialized.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Undertable pawn shop is tucked away enough to avoid drawing attention but is not so remote as to be difficult to reach.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Anzu has a raven companion named Othello.
claims.speaker: Anzu

claims.claim: The Undertable is a narrow, squished shop wedged between two buildings in a space too large for an alleyway but too small for a standard building, with no shop-front windows and only a door marked by a handwritten sign reading 'under table' alongside a crude drawing of a table.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The interior of the Undertable is a cramped room filled with tables stacked high with miscellaneous goods including kitchen appliances, loose jewelry, radios in various states of deconstruction, and a large number of firearms.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler is a centaur and the proprietor of the Undertable, as well as a fence known to Johnny.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler stands approximately nine feet tall at full centaur height and must stoop beneath the ceiling of the Undertable.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler extends a friends discount to associates of Johnny as a personal favor, on the basis that friends of his friend will not inform on him to law enforcement.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler operates as a fence and deals in both buying and selling goods.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler's pawn shop carries poly tools, priced at nine gold pieces.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Belvedere is a place where children receive an education, functioning as a school among other things.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Children in Belvedere do not ordinarily receive permission to travel hundreds of miles away from Belvedere into the field, making such cases special exceptions.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Johnny's home country of Raelion no longer exists, having been destroyed.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Benny possesses a glowing pick that was empowered by an entity called Eternal Pulse.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Argyle's parents are named Aaron Cross and Joran Davinrock, and they have different last names.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Argyle knows all members of the Scale by name.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch Scrap is a scrapyard located in the Sableclutch neighborhood.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The city of Hallia has a public tram transportation system.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch is not a prosperous part of town.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch is notable for having a large number of religious areas, including many churches.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The churches in Sableclutch are mostly dedicated to Iridescent Host, with a smattering of other gods also represented.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The scrapyard is located on the voidward side of Hallia, reachable by tram.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The voidward strata of Hallia contains slums between the city center and the scrapyard.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The voidward side of Hallia is dominated by factories that deliberately locate near the river running through Hallia in order to dump waste without legal or public-health consequences.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A river runs through the city of Hallia.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch Scrap is a large, expansive junkyard featuring mountains of trash and industrial equipment including a crane-like device used to flatten vehicles.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The scrapyard on the voidward side of Hallia is named Sableclutch Scrap.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A contact named Copperjaw is associated with Sableclutch Scrap and is known to the character Anouk.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw is a dwarven man who operates a Crusher.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw has a mashed-up, misshapen nose and a black eye, indicating he continues to get into fights.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw appears to be at the end of middle age or beginning of elderly by dwarven standards, estimated to be around 120 to 130 years old.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw's bottom jaw has been replaced with a crude copper prosthetic, which is the origin of his name.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw offers work in two categories: guarding something or moving something, with moving paying better at 100 gold pieces.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw operates a converted storage container near the front of his scrapyard that serves as a small office.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw possesses a locked metal suitcase fitted with a magic handcuff on its handle, used to secure the case to a courier during delivery jobs.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The metal suitcase offered by Copperjaw is extraordinarily heavy — heavy enough that dropping it would make a small crater in the ground.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw is able to deter the two interested parties when they come to his scrapyard, partly because he has a Giant crusher available there, but does not feel confident protecting the suitcase outside of his scrapyard.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Diamond District is a relatively upscale part of the city, known for jewelers and fine metal shops, and is located near high-end residential areas.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The setting does not have television; radio shows exist instead.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Tarv is a cross-sphere entity or concept, meaning it exists across multiple spheres/worlds.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Tarv, a comic character featuring a gun arm, is said to arise independently in every society that has both advanced entertainment and advanced firearms, described as convergent evolution across spheres.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The setting is described as a magical society where seeing bird people and similar non-human beings is commonplace and does not attract unusual attention.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Threshold Authority officers wear a greenish uniform with a large 'TA' insignia on it.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Black Line is a law enforcement organization that generally polices the nicer parts of the city.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Black Line officers wear full uniforms and carry batons as their standard sidearm, rather than firearms.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A law enforcement service called Blackline operates in the setting, and officers who work for it are paid through subscriptions rather than public funding.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline officers only enforce the law for those who pay a subscription fee, and openly acknowledge this as how they make their living.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch Scrap is a junkyard or scrapyard business whose proprietor is known as Copperjaw.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline officers patrol the area around Sableclutch Scrap regularly.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch Scrap has a reputation for giving jobs to anyone willing to do the work.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline badges exist as physical identification carried by Blackline personnel, bearing an identification number.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline does not frequently interact with local law enforcement, making its badges unfamiliar to officers.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline is a private law enforcement company that operates in at least some districts, providing patrol and security services to clients.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw is a named individual known to Blackline operatives, who would be informed if a goblin of interest were caught.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw has been known to hire people, including children, to deliver important items or cases.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Officer Smith is a named officer of the local law enforcement.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Officer Smith's partner, known as 'Mute', is unable to speak due to a bad accident sustained in a scuffle with a local gang.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The area the party is traveling through has relatively winding streets.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The area lacks traffic cops because employing them would cost money, and there is no clear party to fund them.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The area contains factories and scrapyards, with trucks used to cart goods in and out of them.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The area is served by a trolley station.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The local transit system uses trams (also called trolleys) that materialize at stations on a scheduled basis.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Goblinoid creatures are commonly employed as workers in the factories of the area, and can be seen commuting in workman's attire.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Skinwalkers are difficult to identify by sight alone, requiring a significantly higher DC than identifying whether someone is mute through conversation.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Masque District is an area of the city that contains hotels and serves as a tourist destination.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The tram system uses numbered stations, with at least one station designated 'Void 2' and a destination station designated 'Prime' or 'Zero'.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The tram line is operated by Prime Meridian.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The tram stop at void one serves as a central location on its line, with many passengers disembarking there.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The residential area the party is heading toward is wealthy enough that all its inhabitants can afford private cars, making public transport unavailable into that part of the city.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A taxi fare into the wealthy residential district costs approximately three silver pieces.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Two entities known as Smith and Mute are capable of disguising themselves as ordinary office workers to blend into urban environments.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: One of the two entities (referred to as Mute) communicates only through a gargling sound rather than speech, suggesting an inability to speak normally.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smith and Mute are shapeshifting creatures that can grow approximately a foot and a half in size when dropping their disguise.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: In their true form, Smith and Mute appear as large, pink, fleshy, naked mole rat-like creatures whose clothing melts into their bodies.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: In their true form, Smith and Mute have faces where the eyes sink into featureless sockets and most other facial features dissolve back into the skin.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The flesh of the mound creatures is stringy in texture, allowing a hand to be pushed into it, and items can be found embedded within the flesh.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Black line badges can be found concealed inside the flesh of the mound creatures, having been absorbed into their bodies.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster
